verdict.answer_first: A states the core answer immediately: "turning it into a Progressive Web App (PWA) built around a service worker"; B opens with "No worries" before getting there.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to the main path and key split: "read-only or read-write offline"; B surveys many tools and engines, which is useful but less decisive.
verdict.fabrication: A has the weaker unsupported concrete claim: "roughly a day of work"; B also has uncited claims, but less promise-like.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it gives framework-specific starting points and build order: "Next.js", "Vite", "Angular", then "Suggested order to build it".
